response: {"purpose":"Figure 1 introduces the eCLIP-seq methodology as an enhanced approach for identifying RNA binding protein (RBP) target sites with greater accuracy and efficiency compared to the existing iCLIP protocol. The figure walks through the key procedural innovations — including inline barcoding, size-matched input (SMInput) controls, and random-mer-based PCR duplicate removal — and demonstrates how these steps increase the proportion of usable reads retained after library processing. By comparing RBFOX2 eCLIP and iCLIP data through downsampling experiments and read density visualisations, the figure shows that eCLIP yields more reproducible, higher-confidence binding site calls, underscoring the practical value of the method for transcriptome-wide RBP studies.","title":"eCLIP-seq improves RBP target identification over iCLIP"}
